adminApi.error.serviceNotRunning: Service is not running, so the request cannot be sent
adminApi.error.networkUnavailable: Service is not running or the network is unreachable. Check whether the backend has started.
setup.title: CCX Desktop Initial Setup
setup.description: PROXY_ACCESS_KEY is the credential AI agents use to access upstream APIs through the CCX proxy. Every caller must have this key.
setup.hint: After saving, CCX will start automatically. You can adjust other settings later on the Environment page.
webui.notRunning: CCX service is not running, so the Web UI cannot be displayed.
details.title: Service details
channel.title: Channel Center
channel.description: Use DeepSeek, MiMo, Kimi, GLM, SenseNova, MiniMax, DashScope, iFlytek Xinghuo, and more keys for both direct Agent routing and the unified CCX channel pool. Provider presets handle advanced switches automatically.
channel.promo: Register via promotion link to claim the exclusive perk
channel.keySavedPlaceholder: Saved locally; leave empty to reuse this Provider Key
channel.keyInputPlaceholder: Enter API Key. It is stored only in local Desktop config
channel.missingKey: Enter an API Key, or save this Provider key in Agent Config first.
channel.reuseKey: Will reuse the saved local {provider} key: {key}
channel.name: Channel name
channel.nameHint: A channel with the same name will be overwritten. Use another name to create a separate channel.
channel.capabilityHint: Capability toggles: reasoning / vision / model list / compatibility fields are configured by Provider.
copilotOAuth.description: Use GitHub OAuth to authorize Copilot. After authorization, the GitHub OAuth token is added as a key for this channel. This token is only used to communicate with GitHub; CCX automatically exchanges it for a short-lived Copilot token.
copilotOAuth.success: Authorization succeeded. The GitHub OAuth token has been added to this channel.
copilotOAuth.expired: The authorization code has expired. Please restart authorization.
subscription.title: Subscriptions
subscription.description: Manage account-authorized subscription services and add the authorized access as CCX channels.
subscription.copilotDescription: Use GitHub OAuth to authorize Copilot. The same authorization can be added to any Claude, Chat, Codex, or Gemini protocol channel. This token is only used to communicate with GitHub; CCX automatically exchanges it for a short-lived Copilot token.
subscription.addingCopilotAccount: Adding account to the selected channel...
subscription.copilotAuthorizationSavedOn: Authorized. No {target} channel detected — click the button below to add it.
subscription.accountsEmpty: No authorized accounts on the {target} channel yet.
subscription.accountsSummary: {count} authorized account(s) pooled on the {target} channel
subscription.verifyFailed: Could not fetch this GitHub account. Please retry.
subscription.channelResolveFailed: Channel was created but could not be located. Please retry.
subscription.targetClaudeDesc: Add to Messages channels for Claude Code protocol access
subscription.targetChatDesc: Add to OpenAI Chat Completions channels
subscription.targetCodexDesc: Add to Responses channels for Codex protocol access
channel.preset.deepseek.label: DeepSeek
channel.preset.deepseek.description: V4-Pro / V4-Flash dual-flagship reasoning models at breakthrough costs. Fully open-source (MIT), delivering frontier-level performance at unbeatable prices.
channel.preset.mimo.label: MiMo
channel.preset.mimo.description: Xiaomi's in-house AI platform for the Agent era. Flagship MiMo-V2.5-Pro with trillion parameters and 1M context, powering Xiaomi's smart ecosystem across devices, vehicles, and homes.
channel.preset.compshare.label: Youyun Zhisuan Plans
channel.preset.compshare.description: Youyun Zhisuan is UCloud's AI cloud platform, offering cost-effective domestic AI model Agent Plan packages by monthly subscription or pay-as-you-go, starting from 49 CNY/month. It also provides stable access to official overseas models, supports Claude Code, Codex, and API integrations, and offers enterprise-grade high concurrency, 24/7 technical support, and self-service invoicing. Users who register through the promotion link can receive a free 5 CNY platform trial credit.
channel.preset.runapi.label: RunAPI
channel.preset.runapi.description: RunAPI is an efficient and stable API platform—an alternative to OpenRouter. A single API Key gives you access to 150+ leading models, including OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, Grok, and more, at prices as low as 10% of the original (up to 90% off), with exceptional stability. It's seamlessly compatible with tools like Claude Code, OpenClaw, and others. RunAPI offers an exclusive perk for CCX users: register and contact an administrator to claim ¥7 in free credit.
channel.preset.kimi.label: Kimi / Moonshot
channel.preset.kimi.description: Moonshot AI's intelligent assistant platform. Flagship Kimi K2.6 with native multimodal, renowned for ultra-long context and Agent Swarm capabilities.
channel.preset.glm.label: GLM / BigModel
channel.preset.glm.description: Zhipu AI's model-as-a-service platform from Tsinghua. Flagship GLM-5.1 rivals frontier models in coding. Hong Kong-listed, trained entirely on Huawei Ascend chips.
channel.preset.sensenova.label: SenseNova
channel.preset.sensenova.description: SenseTime's SenseNova LLM API platform with OpenAI and Anthropic compatibility. Preset uses GLM-5.2, DeepSeek V4 Flash, and SenseNova 6.7 Flash-Lite as multimodal fallback.
channel.preset.minimax.label: MiniMax
channel.preset.minimax.description: Global multimodal AI leader (HK-listed). Flagship M3 with 1M context, native vision, and Agent capabilities. Hailuo video/audio models in global top tier. Over 70% revenue from international markets.
channel.preset.dashscope.label: Alibaba Cloud DashScope
channel.preset.dashscope.description: Alibaba Cloud's one-stop LLM platform integrating Qwen and third-party models. Full-modality coverage with pay-as-you-go, Coding Plan, and Token Plan options.
channel.preset.opencode-zen.label: OpenCode Zen / Go
channel.preset.opencode-zen.description: OpenCode's curated model gateway with the Go subscription and Zen pay-as-you-go plans sharing the same coding model preset.
channel.preset.opencode-go.label: OpenCode Go
channel.preset.opencode-go.description: OpenCode's low-cost open-source coding model subscription (from $5/month), optimized for coding agent scenarios.
channel.preset.mimo.plan.anthropic.label: Pay-as-you-go (Anthropic)
channel.preset.mimo.plan.token-cn.label: Token Plan - China (OpenAI)
channel.preset.mimo.plan.token-cn.descri: China Token Plan Chat / Responses shared endpoint
channel.preset.mimo.plan.token-sgp.label: Token Plan - Singapore (OpenAI)
channel.preset.mimo.plan.token-sgp.descr: Singapore Token Plan Chat / Responses shared endpoint
channel.preset.mimo.plan.token-ams.label: Token Plan - Europe (OpenAI)
channel.preset.mimo.plan.token-ams.descr: Europe Token Plan Chat / Responses shared endpoint
channel.preset.mimo.plan.token-cn-anthro: China Token Plan Claude Messages native endpoint
channel.preset.mimo.plan.token-sgp-anthr: Singapore Token Plan Claude Messages native endpoint
channel.preset.mimo.plan.token-ams-anthr: Europe Token Plan Claude Messages native endpoint
channel.preset.unity2.label: Unity2.ai
channel.preset.unity2.description: Unity2.ai is a high-performance AI model API relay platform for individual developers, teams, and enterprises. It serves leading domestic enterprises long-term, handles over 30 billion tokens daily, and supports 5000 RPM-level high concurrency. One API Key works seamlessly with Claude Code, Codex, OpenAI models, IDE plugins, and Agent workflows. With enterprise-grade stable supply capability, it maintains low latency and high availability under high concurrency, continuous calling, and team bulk procurement scenarios. Register now to claim $2 balance, join the official group for an additional $10 balance, up to $12 free credits total.
channel.preset.kimi.plan.anthropic.label: Pay-as-you-go (Anthropic)
channel.preset.kimi.plan.coding-anthropi: Coding Plan Claude Messages native endpoint
channel.preset.kimi.plan.coding-openai-c: Coding Plan Chat / Responses shared endpoint
channel.preset.glm.plan.anthropic.label: Anthropic-compatible
channel.preset.glm.plan.coding.label: Coding Plan (OpenAI)
channel.preset.glm.plan.coding.descripti: Coding Plan Chat / Responses shared endpoint
channel.preset.sensenova.plan.openai-cha: Chat endpoint; Responses are converted by CCX
channel.preset.dashscope.plan.coding-ant: Coding Plan Claude Messages native endpoint
channel.preset.dashscope.plan.coding-ope: Coding Plan Chat / Responses shared endpoint
channel.preset.dashscope.plan.token-plan: Token Plan Claude Messages native endpoint
Token Plan Chat / Responses shared endpoint
channel.preset.tencent-lkeap.label: Tencent Cloud TokenHub
channel.preset.tencent-lkeap.description: Tencent Cloud's LLM service platform integrating Hunyuan and mainstream models. Token Plan subscriptions and pay-as-you-go billing, compatible with Claude Code, Codex, and other coding tools.
channel.preset.volc-ark.label: Volcengine Ark Coding Plan
channel.preset.volc-ark.description: ByteDance's one-stop AI platform under Volcengine. Full-chain services for Doubao series models including fine-tuning, inference, and evaluation. Multi-vendor model support with enterprise-grade reliability.
channel.preset.qianfan.label: Baidu Qianfan Coding Plan
channel.preset.qianfan.description: Baidu Cloud's enterprise LLM platform with native ERNIE 5.0 multimodal model. Multi-agent collaboration and full-lifecycle tools from development to deployment.
channel.preset.originrouter.label: OriginRouter
channel.preset.originrouter.description: Unified model API forwarding platform. A single key to seamlessly switch between GPT, Claude, Gemini, and other models. High-speed and stable, ideal for multi-model mixing.
channel.preset.opencode-zen.plan.go-open: Low-cost subscription endpoint. Messages are converted by CCX to the Chat upstream; Chat / Responses use this endpoint directly
channel.preset.opencode-zen.plan.openai-: Pay-as-you-go endpoint. Messages are converted by CCX to the Chat upstream; Chat / Responses use this endpoint directly
channel.preset.opencode-go.plan.openai-c: Messages are converted by CCX to the Chat upstream; Chat / Responses use this endpoint directly
channel.preset.modelscope.label: ModelScope
channel.preset.modelscope.description: Alibaba DAMO Academy's open-source model community with 10,000+ models across text, image, and speech. Full-lifecycle support for model exploration, inference, and training.
channel.preset.xfyun.label: iFlytek Xinghuo
channel.preset.xfyun.description: iFlytek Xinghuo MaaS Astron Coding Plan with separate OpenAI, Anthropic, and Responses Coding Plan endpoints.
channel.preset.openrouter.label: OpenRouter
channel.preset.openrouter.description: The largest AI model aggregator — access hundreds of models with a single API key. OpenAI-compatible with built-in guardrails for security, governance, and cost control.
channel.target.messages.label: Messages native
channel.target.messages.description: Claude Code direct, or CCX messages channel
channel.target.responses.label: Codex Responses
channel.target.chat.label: Chat passthrough
channel.target.chat.description: OpenAI Chat protocol, for Chat clients
env.title: Environment config
env.fieldDisallow: {field} cannot use a sample placeholder value
env.saveSuccess: .env saved; changes take effect after restarting the service
agent.migrateConfirmDesc: This will rewrite Codex local session metadata to the current provider. It updates history, archived sessions, and Codex state database, but never deletes sessions.
agent.codexPlaceholderSaved: Saved locally; leave empty to reuse the key
agent.sessionMigrationWarning: Switching provider mode will make existing sessions invisible in Codex. Sessions are not deleted — switch back to the previous mode to access them.
agent.codexQuickModeHint: Keeps OpenAI conversation history. Uses OpenAI as provider with the configured API endpoint.
agent.codexPluginModeHint: Enables Codex plugins. Previous OpenAI sessions may not be visible — switch back to Quick mode to access them.
agent.hasOwnApiKey: I have my own API key
agent.promo: Register via promotion link to claim the exclusive perk
agent.placeholderSaved: Saved locally; leave empty to reuse the key
agent.placeholderMimo: Required: MiMo API Key (tp-xxx or account key)
agent.placeholderDashScope: Required: DashScope API Key (sk-xxx or sk-sp-xxx)
agent.codexDiagnosticHealthy: Current Codex config looks consistent with CCX.
agent.codexDiagnosticMismatch: config.toml and auth.json may be inconsistent.
agent.codexDiagnosticSuggestedApply: Suggested action: re-apply Codex -> CCX config.
agent.codexDiagnosticSuggestedRestore: If the issue persists, restore first and then apply again.
agent.codexDiagnosticStatusHint: Open the Agent tab to inspect Codex config consistency.
agent.codexDiagnosticCodeMissingApiKey: config.toml points to local CCX, but auth.json is missing OPENAI_API_KEY.
agent.codexDiagnosticCodeProxyKeyMismatc: The proxy key stored in Codex no longer matches the current CCX proxy key.
agent.codexDiagnosticCodeAuthModeMismatc: config.toml and auth.json disagree on the required auth mode.
agent.codexDiagnosticCodeLegacyIncomplet: Legacy ccx provider config is incomplete.
agent.codexDiagnosticCodeConfigPolluted: config.toml may have been polluted by another tool and no longer matches CCX expectations.
agent.codexDiagnosticCodeAuthUnreadable: auth.json cannot be parsed and may be corrupted.
agent.codexDiagnosticCodeModeMismatch: Current Codex config is {currentMode} mode, but you selected {targetMode} mode. Please re-apply the config.
agent.responsesDiagnosticNoChannel: No Responses channel is configured in CCX.
agent.responsesDiagnosticAllDisabled: Responses channels exist, but none is currently active.
agent.responsesDiagnosticNoApiKeys: Active Responses channels are missing API keys.
agent.responsesDiagnosticProtocolMismatc: Responses channels exist, but their protocol routing does not look compatible with /v1/responses.
agent.responsesDiagnosticCircuitOpen: All active Responses channels are currently circuit-open.
agent.responsesDiagnosticSuggestedRefres: Suggested action: refresh channel dashboard and logs.
agent.responsesDiagnosticSuggestedCreate: Suggested action: add a Responses channel for your Codex provider.
agent.responsesDiagnosticSuggestedEnable: Suggested action: enable or resume at least one Responses channel.
agent.responsesDiagnosticSuggestedAddKey: Suggested action: add an API key to an active Responses channel.
agent.responsesDiagnosticSuggestedProtoc: Suggested action: check serviceType and baseUrl so the channel resolves to a /responses endpoint.
agent.responsesDiagnosticSuggestedResume: Suggested action: inspect logs and resume the affected channels after recovery.
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticHealthy: No recent failed Responses requests were found.
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticAuth: Recent Responses requests failed with authentication errors (401/403).
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticRateLimit: Recent Responses requests were rate limited (429).
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticUpstream5xx: Recent Responses requests failed with upstream 5xx errors.
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticTimeout: Recent Responses requests look stalled or timed out.
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticGeneric: Recent Responses requests failed. Check channel logs for details.
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticSuggestedRe: Suggested action: refresh the latest channel logs.
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticSuggestedAu: Suggested action: verify the upstream API key, account quota, and auth settings.
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticSuggestedRa: Suggested action: reduce request rate or wait for the upstream limit window to recover.
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticSuggestedUp: Suggested action: switch channel, inspect upstream status, or retry later.
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticSuggestedTi: Suggested action: inspect upstream latency, timeout settings, and recent logs.
agent.responsesLogsDiagnosticSuggestedGe: Suggested action: open the Responses channel logs and inspect the latest failures.
agent.codexDiagnosticIntro: Troubleshooting runs on demand: config consistency → Responses channel availability → recent failed requests.
env.groupAccessDesc: Access keys for the proxy and admin endpoints.
env.placeholderAdminAccessKey: Leave empty to fall back to PROXY_ACCESS_KEY
env.descAdminAccessKey: Used for the Web UI and /api/* endpoints.
env.groupServerDesc: Desktop injects some runtime values on start; this section still matches .env.example.
env.descPort: Preferred startup port. If occupied, the next available port is used automatically.
env.descBindHost: Leave empty to listen on all interfaces (:PORT / 0.0.0.0). Use 127.0.0.1 for local-only access.
env.descEnableHttps: For clients such as Claude Desktop that only accept HTTPS gateways. WebURL and health checks switch to https when enabled.
env.descTlsAutoCert: Generates a temporary localhost self-signed certificate when cert files are not provided. Use cert files if the client requires a trusted certificate.
env.descTlsCertFile: PEM certificate path. Set together with TLS_KEY_FILE.
env.descTlsKeyFile: PEM private key path. Set together with TLS_CERT_FILE.
env.groupWebUIDesc: Control whether the Web UI is enabled and which language it uses by default.
env.descEnableWebUI: Desktop mode usually forces this on.
env.groupLogsDesc: Control request/response logs, SSE debugging, and response model rewriting.
env.descEnableResponseLogs: Response logs may expose sensitive content.
env.groupPerformanceDesc: Request-chain timeouts and request body size limits.
env.groupCircuitBreakerDesc: Control scheduler metric windows and failure-rate thresholds.
env.runtimeCbDesc: Fine-tune circuit breaker and stream control parameters. Changes take effect immediately.
env.runtimeCbWindowSizeDesc: Number of recent requests for failure rate (3-100)
env.runtimeCbConsecutiveFailuresDesc: Triggers circuit break on N consecutive retryable failures (1-100)
env.runtimeCbStreamFirstContentTimeoutDe: Wait for first valid content after HTTP 200 (0=disabled, 1000-300000ms)
env.runtimeCbStreamInactivityTimeoutDesc: Wait for subsequent activity after first content (0=disabled, 1000-180000ms)
env.runtimeCbStreamToolCallIdleTimeoutDe: Wait for valid output while a tool call is pending (1000-180000ms)
env.runtimeCbSaved: Tuning Bench settings saved. Changes take effect immediately.
env.runtimeCbNoBackend: Backend service not running. Start the service first.
env.runtimeCbServiceStopped: Service is stopped. Start it to configure runtime parameters.
env.groupMetricsPersistenceDesc: Control SQLite metrics persistence and data retention.
logs.empty: No logs yet. Start the service to view output.
diagnostic.binarySuggestionBuild: Confirm the CCX binary has been built: cd backend-go && make build
diagnostic.binarySuggestionCheckDataDir: Check whether ccx-go / ccx-go.exe exists in the Desktop data directory
diagnostic.binarySuggestionDownload: Build the backend first, or download a prebuilt release from the Release page
diagnostic.portSuggestionInstance: Check whether another CCX instance is already running
diagnostic.portSuggestionEnv: Change the PORT field in .env to another port
diagnostic.portSuggestionInspect: Use lsof -i :3688 (macOS/Linux) or netstat -ano | findstr :3688 (Windows) to inspect port usage
diagnostic.healthSuggestionLogs: Check the log panel for startup errors
diagnostic.healthSuggestionChannels: Confirm upstream channel configuration is correct. First startup may take longer.
diagnostic.permissionSuggestionDataDir: Check whether the data directory is writable
diagnostic.permissionSuggestionExecutabl: macOS/Linux: confirm the binary is executable (chmod +x)
diagnostic.genericSuggestionLogs: Check the log panel below for detailed errors
env.saveSuccessHint: .env saved; changes take effect after restarting the service
sidebar.versionHintStore: Microsoft Store version updates automatically via Store
sidebar.versionHintTray: Check for updates from the tray menu
orchestration.noActiveChannels: No channels yet. Click the button above to add one.
orchestration.cacheWriteHighHint: The cache write ratio is high in the last 15 minutes, which may indicate suboptimal cache-hit configuration.
orchestration.deleteActiveGuard: Cannot delete: keep at least one active channel in the failover sequence
channelEditor.basic.name.label: Name
channelEditor.basic.serviceType.label: Upstream type
channelEditor.basic.description.label: Description
channelEditor.basic.baseUrl.label: Base URL
channelEditor.basic.baseUrl.required: At least one Base URL is required
channelEditor.basic.additionalUrls.label: Additional URLs (one per line)
channelEditor.transport.proxyUrl.label: Proxy URL
channelEditor.transport.skipTls.label: Skip TLS verification
channelEditor.auth.keys.label: API Keys
channelEditor.auth.disabledKeys.label: Disabled keys
channelEditor.advanced.authHeader.label: Auth header
channelEditor.advanced.authHeader.hint: Override how the upstream API key is sent; Auto keeps the existing compatibility logic
addChannel.enterApiKey: Enter at least one API key
copilotOAuth.quickAddKeyHint: No pasted API key is required. Sign in with GitHub Copilot after creating the channel.
addChannel.apiKeyLoadBalance: Multiple keys can be added as failover candidates
channelEditor.auth.apiKeyRequired: At least one API Key required
addChannel.supportedModelsPlaceholder: Press Enter after each rule, for example gpt-4*, *image*, or !*image*
addChannel.supportedModelsHint: Matches the original model requested by the client before model redirect is applied. Leave empty to allow all models. Supported forms: exact, prefix*, *suffix, *contains*, and exclusion with !.
addChannel.supportedModelsInvalidPattern: Invalid rule ignored. Only exact, prefix*, *suffix, *contains*, and ! exclusion are allowed.
addChannel.defaultContextWindowHint: Used when the actual model is not covered by the built-in registry or JSON overrides. Empty uses the unknown-model policy.
addChannel.defaultMaxOutputHint: Only filters requests that explicitly set an output limit.
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesJsonHint: Keys use the actual model name after model redirect and may use wildcards. Empty object means no built-in override.
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesJsonInvalid: Enter a valid JSON object. Values may only include contextWindowTokens, maxOutputTokens, thinkingMode, and reasoningEfforts.
addChannel.allowUnknownContextLabel: Allow unknown models for large context
addChannel.allowUnknownContextHint: By default, unknown-capability models are candidates only up to 200K tokens. Enable this to allow larger requests.
addChannel.visionFallbackPlaceholder: Model to use when request contains images
addChannel.visionFallbackHint: When a request contains images and the target model does not support vision, this model will be used instead. Leave empty to failover to the next channel.
addChannel.visionFallbackReasoningHint: Reasoning effort used when image requests switch to the vision fallback model.
channelEditor.mapping.modelFetchNeedsCon: Base URL and API Key required to fetch models
channelEditor.mapping.configured.label: Configured
addChannel.modelHintTip: Click a target model field to load supported upstream models. Detection status for each API key appears in the key list.
addChannel.modelMappingHintChat: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "gpt-4o" to "gpt-5.4".
addChannel.modelMappingHintGemini: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "gemini-pro" to "gemini-3.5-flash".
addChannel.modelMappingHintResponses: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "opus" to "gpt-5.4".
addChannel.modelMappingHintVectors: Configure Embeddings model name mapping to redirect the requested vector model to a target model. Example: redirect "text-embedding-3-small" to "jina-embeddings-v2-base-zh".
addChannel.modelMappingHintMessages: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "opus" to "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929".
addChannel.sourceModelNameTooLong: Custom model name is too long (max 50 characters)
addChannel.sourceModelInvalidChars: Model name can only contain letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores, dots, slashes, colons, @, and +
addChannel.fillBaseUrlAndApiKey: Fill in Base URL and at least one API key first
addChannel.allApiKeysModelsFailed: No API key could load the model list. Check the error shown for each API key.

channelEditor.streamTimeout.inheritHint: Disabled means this channel inherits the global stream timeout

channelEditor.mapping.redirect.title: Model redirect
channelEditor.runtime.title: Runtime
channelEditor.transport.title: Transport
channelEditor.rateLimit.title: Rate Limit
channelEditor.compat.fastMode.hint: Codex /fast mode speeds up generation on supported models for faster coding iteration, but consumes credits or usage limits at a higher rate
channelEditor.compat.vision.title: Vision
channelEditor.compat.autoBlacklistBalanc: Automatically trip/disable keys with balance errors
channelEditor.runtime.autoLearnRateLimit: Parse Retry-After / x-ratelimit-* headers and adjust cooldown dynamically
channelEditor.rateLimit.rpm.label: Max requests per minute (RPM)
channelEditor.rateLimit.window.label: Sliding window (s)
channelEditor.mapping.supportedModels.la: Supported models (whitelist, leave empty for no restriction)
channelEditor.mapping.source.duplicate: This source model already has a redirect
channelEditor.basic.info.title: Basic info
channelEditor.connection.title: Connection
channelEditor.mapping.hint: Intercept source aliases in requests and route to the real upstream target model
channelEditor.compat.visionFallback.hint: Models disabled via the vision toggle on redirect rows will auto-fallback to this model for image requests
channelEditor.compat.noVision.hint: When enabled, requests containing images skip this channel and failover to the next one
channelEditor.compat.historicalImageLimi: 0 means unlimited; when set to 2-10, images beyond the limit are replaced with placeholder
channelEditor.compat.compactModel.label: Compact Model
channelEditor.compat.compactModel.hint: Upstream model name for local compact (compression/summary), bypassing model mapping. Always used for non-Responses protocol channels; for Responses channels, used as fallback when native compact fails.
channelEditor.compat.passbackReasoning.h: Convert historical thinking blocks into OpenAI-style reasoning_content for passback. Claude-protocol upstreams that require reasoning_content (e.g. MiMo, DeepSeek and other Chinese first-party APIs) need this on; standard Anthropic endpoints or full-featured relays usually don't. Run Diagnose to auto-detect.
channelEditor.compat.passbackThinking.hi: Project real reasoning_content back into content[].thinking. Upstreams that strictly validate Claude thinking blocks (e.g. DeepSeek, GLM and other Chinese first-party APIs) need this on; standard Anthropic endpoints or full-featured relays usually don't. Run Diagnose to auto-detect.
channelEditor.compat.stripThoughtSignatu: Remove the thought_signature field from functionCall
channelEditor.compat.injectDummySignatur: Inject a dummy signature into functionCall for third-party APIs
channelEditor.compat.reasoningStyle.hint: Choose whether to use reasoning.effort or reasoning_effort for OpenAI-style requests
channelEditor.compat.codexNativeTools.hi: Convert Codex native tools into OpenAI function format
channelEditor.compat.stripImageGen.hint: Remove image generation tool calls from requests
channelEditor.compat.stripEmptyBlocks.hi: Remove the bare empty text block Claude Code inserts before tool_use. Strict first-party APIs of Chinese models (e.g. DeepSeek, GLM) often return 400 because of it and usually need this on; standard Anthropic endpoints or lenient relays usually don't. Run Diagnose to auto-detect.
channelEditor.compat.normalizeSystem.hin: Extract system messages from the messages array back to the top-level system field. Native first-party APIs of Chinese models (DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, MiniMax, etc.) often accept only user/assistant roles and usually need this on; standard Anthropic endpoints or full-featured relays usually don't. Run Diagnose to auto-detect.
channelEditor.transport.lowQuality.hint: Mark this channel as low quality, used as last resort
channelEditor.runtime.autoBlacklist.hint: Automatically trip/disable keys with balance errors
channelEditor.transport.skipTls.hint: Disable TLS certificate verification for upstream
channelEditor.transport.requestTimeout.h: Total timeout for non-streaming upstream requests; leave empty to inherit global
channelEditor.transport.responseHeaderTi: Wait after connection for upstream HTTP headers; relax per slow-start channel only
channelEditor.rateLimit.section.label: Rate limiting
channelEditor.rateLimit.section.hint: Proactively throttle requests before they reach the upstream to avoid 429 errors.
channelEditor.rateLimit.burst.label: Burst
channelEditor.rateLimit.burst.hint: Token bucket burst capacity. Auto if empty.
addChannel.customHeadersHint: Add or override HTTP headers sent to the upstream service.
addChannel.streamTimeoutOverrideHint: Custom value overrides the global stream timeout for this channel.
addChannel.streamTimeoutInheritHint: Disabled means this channel inherits the global stream timeout.
addChannel.skipTlsHint: Enable temporarily only for self-signed certificates or domain mismatch. Keep disabled in production.
addChannel.lowQualityHint: When enabled, token usage is force-estimated locally and the local value is used if the deviation exceeds 5%.
addChannel.quickHint: Paste Base URL, API Key, or full config snippet to auto-detect and fill the form.
addChannel.fullSubtitle: Configure channel, keys, models and advanced protocol options
cockpit.empty: No active conversations. Conversations will appear on the radar after requests pass through the gateway.
cockpit.overrideHint: Select the channel sequence for this conversation (by priority):
cockpit.overrideRequired: Please select at least one channel
cockpit.tooltip.quickSetNext: Pin as priority channel for this conversation

channelEditor.timeout.hint: All values are in seconds; disabled controls inherit global settings
console.form.headersNoteInject: Custom headers are injected into every upstream request
console.form.headersNoteOverride: Default headers can be overridden, including User-Agent and Authorization
console.form.headersNoteTemplate: Header names and values support template variables when backend substitution is available
capability.noModelsFound: No models found. Add models manually below.
capability.noApiKeyError: This channel has no API key configured and cannot run capability tests.
addChannel.quickInputPlaceholder:
Paste config snippet to auto-detect Base URL and API Key (multi-line supported)
https://api.example.com
https://api-backup.example.com
sk-ant-api03-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
sk-ant-api03-yyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy
addChannel.modelListEmptyHint: No model candidates yet. Fill in Base URL and API key, then focus a model input to load /models automatically.
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesRowsHint: Model redirect targets are added here automatically with built-in presets when available. You can still add passthrough or unmapped models manually.
addChannel.modelCapabilityModelPlacehold: Select or enter an upstream model
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesRowsInvalid: Check model capability rows. Model names are required; token and price values must be non-negative.
addChannel.embeddingCompatibilityHint: Keys use the actual upstream model name after model redirect. Fallback only stays within the same embeddingSpaceId/actual model, dimensions, and normalization semantics.
addChannel.embeddingCapabilityModelPlace: Select or enter an upstream embedding model
addChannel.embeddingSpaceIdPlaceholder: Empty uses the actual model name
addChannel.embeddingCapabilitiesRowsInva: Check embedding compatibility rows. Actual model is required; dimensions and supported dimensions must be positive integers.
channelDiscovery.title: Discover usable config
channelDiscovery.hint: Probe models, protocols, redirects, and compatibility switches with the current Base URL and API key.
channelDiscovery.reasoningNote: Reasoning effort is a role-based default recommendation. Discovery does not test every effort level.
channelDiscovery.applied: Discovery recommendation applied. Review and save.
autopilot.costPreferenceDesc.cost_first: Prioritize low-cost channels, tolerating some quality decrease.
autopilot.costPreferenceDesc.custom: Use custom savings and quality multipliers.
autopilot.costPreferenceDesc.quality_fir: Prioritize high-quality providers, tolerating higher costs.
autopilot.modeDesc.assist: Assist mode: reorders candidate list but does not change the final selection.
autopilot.modeDesc.auto: Fully automatic mode: filters and reorders, takes over scheduling after meeting admission criteria.
autopilot.modeDesc.off: Smart routing is completely disabled. No traces are computed or recorded.
autopilot.modeDesc.shadow: Only computes and records routing decisions without affecting real scheduling (recommended).
autopilot.modePanel.confirmMessage: About to switch to {mode} mode. This mode will affect real scheduling. Do you want to continue?
autopilot.modePanel.killSwitchActive: Kill switch is active. Smart routing is forced off. Disable the kill switch first to use other modes.
autopilot.modePanel.killSwitchHint: When enabled, forces smart routing off. Takes priority over routing mode selection.
